summary: Large decomp-matching PR across fighter, item, stage, game-mode, menu, toy, and lb audio code. The bot report records +9176 matched-code bytes, +0.24% overall matched code, 69 new matches, 14 unmatched-item improvements, and 2 unmatched-item regressions. Review initially requested fixing regressions relative to both master and a pre-merge commit; final review approved after the remaining regressions were characterized as more or less false positives.

what_changed: Replaced numerous decomp placeholder comments with C implementations across 68 files and several subsystems.
Added or corrected header prototypes, including converting ftKb_SpecialN_800EF040 from UNK_RET to void with typed parameters and adding address-annotated declarations in ftCo_Attack100.h.

Used scoped #pragma push/#pragma dont_inline on/#pragma pop blocks and PAD_STACK in selected fighter functions, notably ftCo_800DA698, ftKb_SpecialN_800F0FC0, ftKb_SpecialN_800F130C, and ftCh_Fail_Anim.
Recovered a natural counted loop in ftKb_SpecialN_800EF040 over Fighter_804D6540 parts, gated by bits from hat->hat_dynamics[1].
Decompiled Kirby copy-ability collision helpers and a Fox/Falco special-N index helper; the latter intentionally used raw motion IDs with a TODO about the ftKb_MS enum mismatch.
Bot report recorded 69 new 100% matches and 14 improvements in unmatched items, while also reporting two .sdata2 unmatched-item regressions.
Review required checking regressions against both master and a pre-merge commit; final approval stated the last regressions were more or less false positives.

smart_moves.text: Kept matching pragmas scoped with push/pop rather than using broad file-level optimizer changes.
Updated the owning header when a function body proved a more specific signature, as shown by ftKb_SpecialN_800EF040 changing from UNK_RET to void with explicit parameters.
Preserved an explicit TODO where enum names were not safe to use because of a known ftKb_MS enum mismatch, avoiding an unsupported semantic cleanup.
Regression review compared against both current master and a pre-merge commit before approval.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When recovering a function body proves a signature, update the owning header instead of leaving UNK_RET or vague parameters.
Bitmask-driven repeated operations can often be expressed as a natural counted loop over a parts table, as in ftKb_SpecialN_800EF040.
If enum definitions are known to be mismatched, raw numeric motion IDs plus explanatory comments/TODO may be safer than falsely using enum names.

For large decomp batches, compare report regressions against both the merge base/master and the immediate pre-merge baseline; some remaining fuzzy regressions may be false positives, but they should be explicitly reviewed.

naming_conventions.text: Address-style fn_ names were retained for functions without proven semantics, while established semantic names such as ftCo_CatchAttack_Anim, ftKb_CaSpecialN_Coll, and ftCh_Fail_Anim were used where already implied by surrounding motion-state naming.
Short local role names such as fp, gobj, parts, joint, mask, and msid were used in recovered fighter code.
Header declarations remained address-commented and ordered around known function offsets.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Scoped #pragma dont_inline blocks were used around selected functions to preserve matching codegen.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk_note: Loaded evidence shows scoped push/pop usage but does not include per-function objdiff details beyond bot report matches and review status.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: PAD_STACK was used narrowly in several recovered functions, including ftCo_800DA698, ftKb_SpecialN_800F0FC0, ftKb_SpecialN_800F130C, ftKb_CaSpecialN_Coll, ftKb_CaSpecialAirN_Coll, and ftCh_Fail_Anim.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk_note: Use aligns with matching-tactic evidence expectations only because the PR has bot/report review evidence; the slice does not show individual objdiff commands.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Direct gobj->user_data access was used in some functions instead of GET_FIGHTER, likely as a codegen-sensitive access shape.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk_note: The PR evidence supports that this access shape was present, but not the author's full rationale.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: A self-assignment form `Fighter* fp = fp = gobj->user_data` remained in Kirby hat-loading functions with an existing note that self-assignment was required for register allocation.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk_note: This is a narrow matching tactic; do not generalize without verifier evidence.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.text: Using `ninja baseline` and `ninja changes`, fix all regressions relative to both master and the pre-merge commit; listed regressions included ftKb_Init, gmmultiman, fn_801697FC, gm_8016A164, fn_801F5914, mnDiagram_80242C0C, itfreeze, ithitodeman, fn_80393C14, and hsd_80393DA0.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.text: Final review said the last regressions were more or less false positives.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.text: Changes against master were good to go; remaining pre-merge comparison listed fuzzy drops for gm_8016A164, fn_801F5914, mnDiagram_80242C0C, fn_80393C14, and hsd_80393DA0.

follow_up_queries: ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FDD4C raw motion IDs enum TODO

notes: PR metadata, changed-file list, bot report, and review comments are loaded and directly support identity, scope, changed files, and review history.
The raw diff is truncated, so file-specific details are strongest for files present in the excerpt and weaker for later changed files.
No inline review comments were present in the slice.
The bot report gives aggregate matching progress and named matches but does not show individual build commands or objdiff output for every changed function.
Author field differs by context: PR metadata/raw PR identify stephenjayakar as PR author, while the human excerpt is a ribbanya issue comment.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Regression review can require both master and pre-merge baselines
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR 2184, review explicitly required `ninja baseline` and `ninja changes` and listed regressions relative to both master and a pre-merge commit before later approving the remaining regressions as more or less false positives.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Header signature updates should accompany recovered bodies
accepted_candidate_records.text: ftKb_SpecialN_800EF040 was implemented as a void function taking Fighter_GObj*, int, and KirbyHatStruct*, and the owning header was updated from UNK_RET to that signature.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Keep enum uncertainty explicit
accepted_candidate_records.text: ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FDD4C used raw motion IDs and comments with a TODO to use enum values once the ftKb_MS enum is fixed, avoiding an unsupported enum cleanup in the PR.

source_update_candidates.title: Track ftKb_MS enum mismatch noted in ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FDD4C
source_update_candidates.text: The PR diff includes `@todo Use enum values once ftKb_MS enum is fixed (#ftCo_MS_Count mismatch)` above ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FDD4C. Curator may want to link this to an existing enum cleanup issue or path note rather than treating the raw motion IDs as preferred style.
source_update_candidates.reason: This is a local TODO/source fact candidate, not a global decomp standard.

source_update_candidates.title: Consider adding baseline/pre-merge regression comparison guidance
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2184 review used both current master and a pre-merge commit as regression baselines, then approved after remaining regressions were deemed false positives. Existing verification standards cover narrow build/objdiff evidence, but curator may consider whether to add explicit guidance for multi-baseline regression review on large decomp batches.
source_update_candidates.reason: The PR evidence supports the practice in this case, but a curator should decide whether it is broad enough for a global standard.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that all remaining regressions are generally acceptable; this PR only records reviewer approval that the last regressions in this case were more or less false positives.
Do not promote PAD_STACK, gobj->user_data, self-assignment, or dont_inline as normal style; the slice only supports them as narrow matching tactics in this PR.
Do not claim all 68 changed files are fully reviewed at diff-detail level; the loaded diff excerpt is truncated.
